title: Safe Harbor Entry
description: Defines a known-correct implementation pattern. When the scanner matches a finding against a safe harbor entry at or above suppression_threshold confidence, the finding is not suppressed silently — it is downgraded and annotated so the developer knows the pattern was checked and considered correct.

id.description: Unique entry ID. Format: {stack}-{category-abbrev}-{NNN}. Example: supabase-secrets-001

schema_version.description: Version of this schema the entry was written against.

title.description: Short human-readable name for the pattern. Should complete the sentence 'Correct implementation of...'

stacks.description: Stack tags this entry applies to. Matched against scan inventory.

category.description: Scoring category this entry belongs to. Maps directly to scorecard domains.

owasp_refs.description: Authoritative OWASP sources this entry was derived from.

section.description: Specific section or anchor if applicable.

source_version.description: Version or date stamp of the upstream source this entry was derived from. Used to detect staleness.

last_reviewed_at.description: ISO 8601 date of last human review.

review_cadence.description: How often this entry should be reviewed for upstream changes.

description.description: Plain-language explanation of what this pattern governs and why it matters. Loaded into the model prompt verbatim — write for a senior engineer.

correct_pattern.description: Narrative description of what a correct implementation looks like. The model uses this as the reference when evaluating a match.

correct_examples.description: One or more concrete code examples of a correct implementation.

code.description: Code snippet demonstrating the correct pattern.

notes.description: Optional annotation on what makes this correct.

incorrect_variants.description: Common incorrect implementations that superficially resemble the correct pattern. Helps the model distinguish a true match from a near-miss.

context_requirements.description: What the scanner must be able to see to make a confident match. If any requirement cannot be verified, confidence is capped at medium.

disqualifiers.description: Signals that override a safe harbor match even if the correct pattern appears present. Any single disqualifier prevents suppression regardless of confidence.

confidence_weight.description: Maximum confidence the scanner can assign to a match on this entry (0.0–1.0). Reflects inherent ambiguity in the pattern — some correct implementations are harder to verify than others.

suppression_threshold.description: Minimum confidence required to annotate a finding as safe-harbor-matched and downgrade severity. Below this threshold the finding surfaces normally. Must be <= confidence_weight.

context_sensitivity.description: How much surrounding context is required to verify this pattern. LOW = local to one file. MEDIUM = spans a few files. HIGH = requires understanding of request lifecycle or system architecture.

notes.description: Reviewer notes, edge cases, known limitations of this entry.
